specification.topic-statement: The input buffer layout system calculates and caches the number of physical terminal rows occupied by the input buffer's text content.

scope.in-scope: Computing physical line count from logical text lines and terminal width, with dirty-flag-based caching; display width calculation for UTF-8 text with ANSI escape sequence exclusion; layout invalidation.
scope.boundary: This topic reads the current text content and byte length from the input buffer. It does not modify the text.
This topic delegates ANSI CSI escape sequence detection to the ANSI parsing system to obtain a byte skip count for each sequence encountered.

behavior.invalidate-layout: The layout cache is marked dirty. Any subsequent call to ensure layout will trigger a full recalculation regardless of whether the terminal width has changed.
behavior.ensure-layout:
The cached physical line count is updated to reflect the current text content and terminal width.
If the layout is not dirty and the cached terminal width equals the supplied terminal width, the operation returns immediately without recalculation.
If the text is empty (null or zero length), the physical line count is set to zero. The cached width is updated and the dirty flag is cleared.
The text is scanned one byte at a time. At each newline character or at the end of the text, the display width of the preceding segment (from the previous boundary to the current position) is computed, and the number of physical rows that segment occupies is added to a running total.
Display width is calculated by iterating through the segment:
- ANSI CSI escape sequences are detected and skipped entirely; they contribute zero width. - Each remaining UTF-8 codepoint is decoded. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are treated as one column each, and decoding advances by one byte. - Valid codepoints contribute their Unicode display width (as defined by the Unicode standard for terminal display), which may be 0, 1, or 2.
The total display width of the segment is the sum of all column widths.
Given a segment's display width and the terminal width:
- A segment with zero byte length (an empty line, i.e. consecutive newlines or a leading newline) contributes exactly 1 physical row. - A segment with zero display width (contains only zero-width characters) contributes exactly 1 physical row. - Otherwise, the number of physical rows is the ceiling of (display_width / terminal_width), computed as integer division with rounding up: (display_width + terminal_width - 1) / terminal_width.
If terminal_width is zero or negative, the segment contributes exactly 1 physical row.
After all segments are processed, the total physical line count is stored in the cache. The cached terminal width is updated to the supplied width, and the dirty flag is cleared.
behavior.get-physical-lines: Returns the cached physical line count. The returned value reflects the last completed call to ensure layout; no recalculation is performed.
